title: uoprprms
text:
uoprprms Return the number of parameters in the specified operation. Explain this code int uoprprms (UHACT hOpr, long* pNum); Copy Parameters hOpr The handle to the operation, as returned by uinstopr pNum (Output) The number of parameters in the operation Return Values For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description uoprprms returns the number of parameters in the operation identified by hOpr . This is useful if you want to loop through all the parameters, examining them one-by-one. You can use the functions uprmdir , uprmtype , and uniname to get information about a specific parameter. This makes it possible to dynamically call Uniface operations. Example: uoprprms, uniname, uprmdir, and uprmtype The following routine prints out a list of the parameters of an arbitrary operation in an arbitrary component. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> char *types[] = { "Unknown", "String", "Boolean", "Numeric", "Float", "Date", "Time", "DateTime","LinearDate", "LinearTime", "LinearDateTime", "Raw", "Image", "Entity", "Occurrence", "IncludedEntity", "Handle" }; char *dires[] = { "Unknown", "In", "Out", "InOut" }; void inspect_operation_params (char *svcname, char *oprname) { UHACT hEnv, hIn, hOp, hParm; long i, nParams; int pType, pDir; char name[1+UPRMNAMESIZE]; char* template1 = " %3s %-20s %-20s %s
"; char* template2 = " %3d %-20s %-20s %s
"; ueopen(&hEnv); uinstnew(hEnv, (UCHAR*)svcname, 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, 0); uinstopr(hIn, (UCHAR*)oprname, 0, &hOp); printf("Parameters of operation %s in service %s:

", oprname, svcname); printf(template1, "Num", "Name ", "Type ", "Direction"); printf(template1, "===", "====================", "====================", "========="); uoprprms(hOp, &nParams); for ( i=1; i<=nParams; i++ ) { uprmdir(hOp, i, &pDir); uprmgeth(hOp, i, 0, &hParm); uprmtype(hParm, &pType); uniname(hOp, i, UPRMNAMESIZE, name); printf(template2, i, name, types[pType], dires[pDir]); } printf("
"); uinstdel(hIn, 0); ufreeh(&hOp); ufreeh(&hIn); ufreeh(&hEnv); } Copy Here is an example of output generated by this function: Explain this code Parameters of operation MYOP in service TESTSVC: Num Name Type Direction === ==================== ==================== ========= 1 P1 Numeric In 2 P2 String InOut 3 P3 Date Out Copy Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise. uprmdir Return the direction (in/out/inout) of the specified parameter. uprmgeth Get the handle of the specified operation parameter or entity field. Or detach a parameter from an operation. uprmtype Return the data type of the specified parameter or entity field. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
